payload.title: Browser UX under HTTPS: CA trust + clear messaging
payload.description:
Browser still uses bearer-token cookie auth (unchanged), but now over HTTPS with a CA the browser doesn't trust by default. On first `scope serve`, print clear instructions for trusting the CA in macOS Keychain (`security add-trusted-cert` invocation), or offer `scope ca trust` to do it for them (with sudo prompt). Otherwise every browser visit shows a red cert warning.

Acceptance:
- First-run output explains the cert warning the user will see
- `scope ca trust` adds ~/.scope-hub/ca/ca.crt to the System keychain as a trusted root (asks for sudo)
- `scope ca untrust` reverses it
- README updated with the bookmark + trust flow

Depends on the CA and HTTPS stories.
